turns.user_message: I was just sitting at my kitchen table staring at an unfinished project, and it completely hit me how slow I am. A few years ago, I would have just brushed it off and kept going without a second thought. Now I just sit there feeling completely stuck while my own brain tells me I am wasting time. It is really frustrating because I know I used to handle these little roadblocks with ease. I just feel like my confidence has completely evaporated over something so simple.

That same feeling keeps creeping back every time I try to finish anything, and it is starting to ruin my whole week. I catch myself staring at the screen for hours, paralyzed by the fear that whatever I produce will just be mediocre. It used to be a passing annoyance, but now it feels like a heavy weight I carry everywhere I go. I keep replaying every little hesitation in my head until I convince myself I am just not capable of doing things right. The doubt is so loud that I can barely focus on the actual task anymore.

I honestly feel like I am drowning in my own hesitation because nothing I attempt ever feels good enough. Every single step forward just highlights how fundamentally flawed my process is compared to how it used to be. I look at that same unfinished work and I just see a giant reflection of my own inadequacy staring back at me. It is exhausting to constantly battle this voice that tells me I will never measure up to my own past standards. I just want to disappear into the background because I am so tired of disappointing myself.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This used to not bother the speaker.
